1	//! Initialization of standard libraries for Lua.
2	//!
3	//! Opens all standard libraries via `require`-style loading and registers
4	//! them into the global table.
5	//!
6	//! Port of `src/linit.c` (66 lines, 1 function).
7	
8	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
9	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
10	
11	// C: lua_CFunction — fn pointer type for Lua-callable C functions.
12	// Matches types.tsv: lua_CFunction → fn(&mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError>
13	type LuaCFunction = fn(&mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError>;
14	
15	// ── Library-name byte-string constants ────────────────────────────────────
16	//
17	// These replace the C macros from lualib.h and lauxlib.h:
18	//   LUA_GNAME        = "_G"         (lauxlib.h)
19	//   LUA_LOADLIBNAME  = "package"    (lualib.h)
20	//   LUA_COLIBNAME    = "coroutine"  (lualib.h)
21	//   LUA_TABLIBNAME   = "table"      (lualib.h)
22	//   LUA_IOLIBNAME    = "io"         (lualib.h)
23	//   LUA_OSLIBNAME    = "os"         (lualib.h)
24	//   LUA_STRLIBNAME   = "string"     (lualib.h)
25	//   LUA_MATHLIBNAME  = "math"       (lualib.h)
26	//   LUA_UTF8LIBNAME  = "utf8"       (lualib.h)
27	//   LUA_DBLIBNAME    = "debug"      (lualib.h)
28	//
29	// Per PORTING.md §3.1 all Lua string data uses &[u8], not &str.
30	
31	// C: static const luaL_Reg loadedlibs[] = {
32	//   {LUA_GNAME, luaopen_base},
33	//   {LUA_LOADLIBNAME, luaopen_package},
34	//   {LUA_COLIBNAME, luaopen_coroutine},
35	//   {LUA_TABLIBNAME, luaopen_table},
36	//   {LUA_IOLIBNAME, luaopen_io},
37	//   {LUA_OSLIBNAME, luaopen_os},
38	//   {LUA_STRLIBNAME, luaopen_string},
39	//   {LUA_MATHLIBNAME, luaopen_math},
40	//   {LUA_UTF8LIBNAME, luaopen_utf8},
41	//   {LUA_DBLIBNAME, luaopen_debug},
42	//   {NULL, NULL}
43	// };
44	//
45	// PORT NOTE: C sentinel `{NULL, NULL}` dropped — Rust slices carry their
46	//   own length, so no terminator is needed.
47	//
48	// PORT NOTE: Per PORTING.md §7, `luaopen_X` → `open` inside the module
49	//   (e.g. `crate::base::open`, `crate::string_lib::open`).  As of Phase A
50	//   the individual stdlib modules exported inconsistent names:
51	//     base.rs        → `pub fn open`          (canonical; matches here)
52	//     string_lib.rs  → `pub fn luaopen_string` (needs rename in Phase B)
53	//     table_lib.rs   → `pub fn open_table`    (needs rename in Phase B)
54	//     math_lib.rs    → `pub fn luaopen_math`  (needs rename in Phase B)
55	//     io_lib.rs      → `pub fn luaopen_io`    (needs rename in Phase B)
56	//     os_lib.rs      → `pub fn open_os`       (needs rename in Phase B)
57	//     utf8_lib, debug_lib, coro_lib, loadlib  → not yet ported (Phase B)
58	//   Phase B should rename every stdlib opener to `pub fn open` and update
59	//   this table accordingly.
60	static LOADED_LIBS: &[(&[u8], LuaCFunction)] = &[
61	    (b"_G",         crate::base::open),
62	    (b"package",    crate::loadlib::luaopen_package),
63	    (b"coroutine",  crate::coro_lib::open_coroutine),
64	    (b"table",      crate::table_lib::open_table),
65	    (b"io",         crate::io_lib::luaopen_io),
66	    (b"os",         crate::os_lib::open_os),
67	    (b"string",     crate::string_lib::luaopen_string),
68	    (b"math",       crate::math_lib::luaopen_math),
69	    (b"utf8",       crate::utf8_lib::open_utf8),
70	    (b"debug",      crate::debug_lib::open_debug),
71	];
72	
73	// C: LUALIB_API void luaL_openlibs (lua_State *L) {
74	//   const luaL_Reg *lib;
75	//   /* "require" functions from 'loadedlibs' and set results to global table */
76	//   for (lib = loadedlibs; lib->func; lib++) {
77	//     luaL_requiref(L, lib->name, lib->func, 1);
78	//     lua_pop(L, 1);  /* remove lib */
79	//   }
80	// }
81	//
82	// PORT NOTE: `LUALIB_API` → `pub` (PORTING.md §4.1 / macros.tsv).
83	//   `luaL_requiref(L, name, func, 1)` → `state.require_lib(name, func, true)?`
84	//   The final `1` argument means "set global" — the loaded module value is
85	//   assigned to the global table under `name` and the value left on the
86	//   stack is then discarded by `lua_pop(L, 1)`.
87	//   `lua_pop(L, 1)` → `state.pop_n(1)` (macros.tsv).
88	/// Open all standard Lua libraries into `state`, registering each into the
89	/// global table.
90	///
91	/// Corresponds to `luaL_openlibs` in `linit.c`.
92	pub fn open_libs(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
93	    for &(name, func) in LOADED_LIBS {
94	        // C: luaL_requiref(L, lib->name, lib->func, 1);
95	        state.require_lib(name, func, true)?;
96	        // C: lua_pop(L, 1);  /* remove lib */
97	        state.pop_n(1);
98	    }
99	    Ok(())
100	}
101	
102	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
103	// PORT STATUS
104	//   source:        src/linit.c  (66 lines, 1 function)
105	//   target_crate:  lua-stdlib
106	//   confidence:    high
107	//   todos:         1
108	//   port_notes:    3
109	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
110	//   notes:         Trivial file. Cross-crate refs (state.require_lib,
111	//                  state.pop_n, crate::*::open) resolve in Phase B.
112	//                  Phase B must also reconcile inconsistent open-function
113	//                  names in the existing stdlib modules (see PORT NOTEs).
114	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115

content.content: crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2972:pub fn parse(

2972	pub fn parse(
2973	    state: &mut LuaState,
2974	    dyd: DynData,
2975	    source: &[u8],
2976	    name: &[u8],
2977	    firstchar: i32,
2978	) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
2979	    let source_str = state.intern_str(name)?;
2980	    let envn_str = state.intern_str(lua_lex::LUA_ENV)?;
2981	
2982	    let rest_bytes: Vec<u8> = source.iter().skip(1).copied().collect();
2983	    let z = lua_lex::ZIO::from_bytes(rest_bytes);
2984	
2985	    let lex_ls = lua_lex::LexState {
2986	        current: firstchar,
2987	        linenumber: 1,
2988	        lastline: 1,
2989	        t: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2990	        lookahead: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2991	        fs: None,
2992	        z,
2993	        buff: lua_lex::LexBuffer::new(),
2994	        h: None,
2995	        dyd: None,
2996	        source: source_str.0.clone(),
2997	        envn: envn_str.0.clone(),
2998	    };
2999	
3000	    let mut lexstate = LexState {
3001	        current: lex_ls.current,
3002	        linenumber: lex_ls.linenumber,
3003	        lastline: lex_ls.lastline,
3004	        t: LexToken::default(),
3005	        lookahead: LexToken::default(),
3006	        fs: None,
3007	        dyd,
3008	        source: Some(source_str.clone()),
3009	        envn: Some(GcRef(lex_ls.envn.clone())),
3010	        lex: lex_ls,
3011	    };
3012	    // C: luaX_setinput is the only setup the C parser performs before
3013	    //   `mainfunc`; it does NOT pre-read the first token. `mainfunc` itself
3014	    //   issues the initial `luaX_next` once its prelude (open_func, vararg
3015	    //   marker, _ENV upvalue) is in place.
3016	
3017	    let mut main_proto = Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder());
3018	    main_proto.source = Some(source_str);
3019	    main_proto.is_vararg = true;
3020	    let main_fs = FuncState {
3021	        f: main_proto,
3022	        prev: None,
3023	        bl: None,
3024	        pc: 0,
3025	        lasttarget: 0,
3026	        previousline: 0,
3027	        nk: 0,
3028	        np: 0,
3029	        nabslineinfo: 0,
3030	        firstlocal: 0,
3031	        firstlabel: 0,
3032	        ndebugvars: 0,
3033	        nactvar: 0,
3034	        nups: 0,
3035	        freereg: 0,
3036	        iwthabs: 0,
3037	        needclose: false,
3038	    };
3039	
3040	    mainfunc(&mut lexstate, state, main_fs)
3041	}
3042	
3043	/// Convert a `lua_lex::TokenValue` into the local `parse::TokenValue` flat shape.
3044	///
3045	/// The parser's local `LexState` predates the lex-side enum and uses a flat
3046	/// (r, i, ts) record; this picks out whichever variant the lexer produced.
3047	fn local_token_value(v: &lua_lex::TokenValue) -> TokenValue {
3048	    match v {
3049	        lua_lex::TokenValue::None => TokenValue::default(),
3050	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Float(r) => TokenValue { r: *r, i: 0, ts: None },
3051	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Int(i) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: *i, ts: None },
3052	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Str(s) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: 0, ts: Some(GcRef(s.clone())) },
3053	    }
3054	}
3055	
3056	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3057	// PORT STATUS
3058	//   source:        src/lparser.c  (1968 lines, 95 functions)
3059	//   target_crate:  lua-parse
3060	//   confidence:    medium
3061	//   todos:         184
3062	//   port_notes:    14
3063	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
3064	//   notes:         All 95 functions translated with correct logical structure.
3065	//                  184 TODO(port) stubs for cross-crate calls (lua_code::*,
3066	//                  lua_lex::*, lua_vm state allocation). Key design choices:
3067	//                  BlockCnt/LhsAssign use Option<Box<...>> chains; FuncState
3068	//                  uses Box<LuaProto> (not GcRef) for mutable access during
3069	//                  build. singlevaraux FuncState.prev chain traversal (upvalue
3070	//                  capture across closures) is a known TODO — needs recursive
3071	//                  descent through fs.prev without double-mutable-borrow.

content.content: crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:1362:pub fn load(

1620	    // they return the closure by value, so `f_parser` must push it here.
1621	    // Without this, the caller (`api::load`) sees stale Nil at top-1 and any
1622	    // subsequent `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` fails with "attempt to call a nil
1623	    // value".
1624	    state.check_stack(1)?;
1625	    state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
1626	
1627	    Ok(())
1628	}
1629	
1630	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1631	///
1632	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1633	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1634	    state: &mut LuaState,
1635	    z: ZIO,
1636	    name: &[u8],
1637	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1638	) -> LuaStatus {
1639	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1640	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1641	    state.inc_nny();
1642	
1643	    let mut p = SParser {
1644	        z,
1645	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1646	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1647	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1648	        name: name.to_vec(),
1649	    };
1650	
1651	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1652	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1653	
1654	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1655	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1656	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1657	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1658	
1659	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1660	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1661	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1662	
1663	    // C: decnny(L)
1664	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1665	    state.dec_nny();
1666	
1667	    status
1668	}
1669	
1670	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1671	// PORT STATUS
1672	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)
1673	//   target_crate:  lua-vm
1674	//   confidence:    medium
1675	//   todos:         23
1676	//   port_notes:    13
1677	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
1678	//   notes:         Core call/stack/error machinery translated faithfully.
1679	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1680	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).
1681	//                  Coroutine functions (lua_resume, lua_yieldk, resume, unroll,
1682	//                  etc.) are translated but require Phase E stack-switching to
1683	//                  actually work.  Hook-callback borrow conflict flagged as
1684	//                  TODO(port) in hook() and finish_ccall(); Phase E must solve.
1685	//                  All method calls (check_stack, gc_check_step, get_ci*,
1686	//                  set_ci*, next_ci, etc.) are best-guess stubs to be wired
1687	//                  up in Phase B once the LuaState API is finalised.
1688	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1689

1540	// Protected parser
1541	// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1542	
1543	/// Parser invocation data passed through `pcall`.
1544	///
1545	/// C: `struct SParser { ZIO *z; Mbuffer buff; Dyndata dyd; const char *mode; const char *name; }`
1546	///
1547	/// PORT NOTE: `const char *mode` and `const char *name` become owned byte vecs
1548	/// so that `SParser` can outlive the original string data without raw pointers.
1549	struct SParser {
1550	    z: ZIO,
1551	    /// LexBuffer from `crate::zio` (Mbuffer in C).
1552	    buff: LexBuffer,
1553	    /// TODO(phase-b): real Dyndata lives in the lua-parse crate.
1554	    dyd: DynDataStub,
1555	    // C: const char *mode — byte slice for chunk mode ("b", "t", or "bt")
1556	    // PORT NOTE: stored as Option<Vec<u8>> to own the bytes; None means no mode restriction.
1557	    mode: Option<Vec<u8>>,
1558	    // C: const char *name — chunk name (source identifier)
1559	    name: Vec<u8>,
1560	}
1561	
1562	/// Checks that the chunk mode permits loading the given kind ("binary" or "text").
1563	///
1564	/// C: `static void checkmode(lua_State *L, const char *mode, const char *x)`
1565	fn check_mode(
1566	    state: &mut LuaState,
1567	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1568	    kind: &[u8],
1569	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1570	    if let Some(mode_bytes) = mode {
1571	        // C: strchr(mode, x[0]) == NULL  — mode doesn't contain the first letter of kind
1572	        let kind_char = kind[0];
1573	        if !mode_bytes.contains(&kind_char) {
1574	            // C: luaO_pushfstring + luaD_throw(L, LUA_ERRSYNTAX)
1575	            // TODO(port): &[u8] display — lossy UTF-8 here is acceptable for mode/kind
1576	            // strings which are always ASCII literals ("binary"/"text" and "bt"/"b"/"t").
1577	            return Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
1578	                "attempt to load a {} chunk (mode is '{}')",
1579	                core::str::from_utf8(kind).unwrap_or("?"),
1580	                core::str::from_utf8(mode_bytes).unwrap_or("?"),
1581	            )));
1582	        }
1583	    }
1584	    Ok(())
1585	}
1586	
1587	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1588	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1589	///
1590	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1591	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1592	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1593	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1594	    let c = p.z.getc();
1595	
1596	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1597	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1598	    let cl = if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1599	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1600	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1601	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1602	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1603	        crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?
1604	    } else {
1605	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1606	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1607	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1608	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1609	        parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?
1610	    };
1611	
1612	    // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1613	    debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1614	    // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1615	    func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1616	
1617	    // PORT NOTE: In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` / `luaU_undump` themselves push the
1618	    // closure onto the stack before returning (see lparser.c `luaY_parser`:
1619	    // `setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L);`). In the Rust port
1620	    // they return the closure by value, so `f_parser` must push it here.
1621	    // Without this, the caller (`api::load`) sees stale Nil at top-1 and any
1622	    // subsequent `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` fails with "attempt to call a nil
1623	    // value".
1624	    state.check_stack(1)?;
1625	    state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
1626	
1627	    Ok(())
1628	}
1629

delta.text: The
original error `pcall_k failed: Runtime: attempt to index a nil value
` is gone. Now the test reaches a different blocker — `phase-b: prec

all` todo, which is the next stub in the chain. The original bug is fixed.

##

Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:1378-1386` — `pub fn load
(...)` had a TODO stub that did not set the loaded main-chunk closure's first upvalue (`_
ENV`) to the globals table. After parsing, the closure's `_ENV` upvalue remained `Closed(Nil)`. When the
chunk executed and tried to look up `print` (i.e. index `_ENV`), the VM raised `attempt to index a nil value
` via `vm.rs:535` (`finish_get` → `L

uaError::type_error(&t, "index")`).

**Fix:** Replaced the TODO with a real implementation. Since `Gc
Ref<UpVal>` (= `Rc<UpVal>`) has no interior mutability and the closure's `upvals` Vec lives behind a shared `Rc<Lu
aLClosure>`, I rebuild the closure: clone the proto, swap `upvals[0]` for a fresh `GcRef::new(UpVal::Closed(globals))`, w
rap in a new `GcRef<LuaLClosure>`, and `set_at(top - 1, ...)` to replace the stack slot. The `LuaValue::
Function(LuaClosure::Lua(...))` match now binds by value so we can rebuild without al

ias issues.

**New error:** `[panic] not yet implemented: phase-b: precall` — a separate downstream stub (`
LuaState::precall`) now blocks execution at the call site. The loop will pick this up next.

content.text:
The original error `pcall_k failed: Runtime: attempt to index a nil value` is gone. Now the test reaches a different blocker — `phase-b: precall` todo, which is the next stub in the chain. The original bug is fixed.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:1378-1386` — `pub fn load(...)` had a TODO stub that did not set the loaded main-chunk closure's first upvalue (`_ENV`) to the globals table. After parsing, the closure's `_ENV` upvalue remained `Closed(Nil)`. When the chunk executed and tried to look up `print` (i.e. index `_ENV`), the VM raised `attempt to index a nil value` via `vm.rs:535` (`finish_get` → `LuaError::type_error(&t, "index")`).

**Fix:** Replaced the TODO with a real implementation. Since `GcRef<UpVal>` (= `Rc<UpVal>`) has no interior mutability and the closure's `upvals` Vec lives behind a shared `Rc<LuaLClosure>`, I rebuild the closure: clone the proto, swap `upvals[0]` for a fresh `GcRef::new(UpVal::Closed(globals))`, wrap in a new `GcRef<LuaLClosure>`, and `set_at(top - 1, ...)` to replace the stack slot. The `LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(...))` match now binds by value so we can rebuild without alias issues.

**New error:** `[panic] not yet implemented: phase-b: precall` — a separate downstream stub (`LuaState::precall`) now blocks execution at the call site. The loop will pick this up next.
